files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,77 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "fluentd-3614-fluentd-in-tail-unreadable-file-causes-following-tail-of-file-to-st",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "fluentd: Fluentd in_tail \"unreadable\" file causes \"following tail of <file>\" to stop and no logs pushed",
+    "description": "Fluentd in_tail \"unreadable\" file causes \"following tail of <file>\" to stop and no logs pushed. Community-reported issue.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify fluentd troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your fluentd deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n fluentd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluentd\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluentd -n fluentd --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review fluentd configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant fluentd configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n fluentd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluentd\nkubectl get configmap -n fluentd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=fluentd\n```\n### Describe the bug\n\nAfter a warning of an \"unreadable\" (likely due to rotation), no more logs were pushed (in_tail + pos_file).\nReloading config or restarting fluentd sorts the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Fluentd in_tail \"unreadable\" file causes \"following tail of…",
+        "description": "In #3614 some users reported that in_tail rarely stops tailing. It seems\nit's caused when skipping update_watcher due to unexpected duplicate\npos_file entries. The root cause is unknown yet. To investigate it, show\nmore infomation when it happens. Ideally it should't happen so that log\nlevel \"warn\"\n```yaml\nIf you see such log message, please report it.\nHello, @ashie! We have something similar to: \"no more log pushed\" and found duplication in `pos` file. \nLike this:\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm Fluentd in_tail \"unreadable\" file causes… is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluentd -n fluentd --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n fluentd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "In #3614 some users reported that in_tail rarely stops tailing. It seems\nit's caused when skipping update_watcher due to unexpected duplicate\npos_file entries. The root cause is unknown yet. To investigate it, show\nmore infomation when it happens. Ideally it should't happen so that log\nlevel \"warn\" is desired for it (I can't reproduce this issue yet).\nSame with the title",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "If you see such log message, please report it.\nHello, @ashie! We have something similar to: \"no more log pushed\" and found duplication in `pos` file. \nLike this:",
+        "Config:\n---------",
+        "PS. the same config is working fine in few other instances. I am not sure what was the problem here.\n\nAppreciate your help in advance. \nWe just ran into the same problem again. Without the log line `Skip update_watcher because watcher has been....` showing up.\nNow we only get these:"
+      ]
+    }
+  },

+    },
